description_long: This is a large, amphora-style vase with a classic ancient Greek design, featuring a wide mouth, two robust handles, and a rounded body that tapers to a narrow footed base. The surface is adorned with intricate black-figure artwork depicting mythological or heroic scenes, with figures in dynamic poses and detailed attire. Decorative bands of floral and geometric motifs encircle the neck and shoulder, adding to the ornate appearance. The vase is primarily terracotta with black painted accents, giving it a striking contrast and historical authenticity. The handles are thick and arching, seamlessly integrated into the body, and the rim is slightly flared. The overall craftsmanship suggests it is both decorative and functional, suitable for display in a refined urban or cultural setting.
description: A large, ancient Greek-style amphora vase with black-figure artwork, ornate handles, and decorative bands on a terracotta body.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main mythological scene with two figures in combat, a central shield, and both handles visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlights one handle and a standing figure, with less of the main scene visible and more focus on the decorative motifs.
description_view_2: Rear view displays a group of standing figures in conversation, with both handles and the full decorative band around the neck visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view features another action scene with two figures, one handle, and the continuation of the floral motifs.
attribute_pattern: mythological scenes with geometric and floral motifs

license_info.creator_display_name: The Imaging Center at Smith College
